2605.06494.title: From Token Lists to Graph Motifs: Weisfeiler-Lehman Analysis of Sparse Autoencoder Features
2605.06494.paper_summary: Sparse autoencoders (SAEs) have become central to mechanistic interpretability, decomposing transformer activations into monosemantic features. Yet existing analyses characterise features almost exclusively through top-activating token lists or decoder weight vectors, leaving the higher-order co-occurrence structure shared across features largely unexamined. We introduce a graph-structured representation in which each SAE feature is modelled as a token co-occurrence graph: nodes are the tokens most frequent near strong activations, and edges connect pairs that co-occur within local context windows. A custom WL-style, frequency-binned graph kernel then provides a similarity measure over this structural space. Applied as a proof of concept to features from a large SAE trained on GPT-2 Small and probed with a synthetic mixed-domain corpus, our clustering recovers heuristic motif families (punctuation-heavy patterns, language and script clusters, and code-like templates) that are not recovered by clustering on decoder cosine similarity. A token-histogram baseline achieves higher overall purity, so the contribution of the graph view is complementary rather than dominant: it surfaces structural relationships that token-frequency and decoder-weight views alone do not capture. Cluster assignments are stable across graph-construction hyperparameters and random seeds.
2605.06494.paper_summary_zh: 稀疏自編碼器（SAEs）已成為機械解釋性的核心，將Transformer的激活分解為單義特徵。<lb/>然而，現有的分析幾乎僅通過頂部激活的標記列表或解碼器權重向量來描述特徵，導致特徵之間共享的高階共現結構在很大程度上未被檢查。<lb/>我們引入了一種圖結構表示法，其中每個SAE特徵被建模為一個標記共現圖：節點是強激活附近最頻繁的標記，而邊則連接在局部上下文窗口內共現的標記對。<lb/>然後，一個自定義的WL風格、頻率分箱的圖核提供了這一結構空間的相似性度量。<lb/>作為概念驗證，應用於從在GPT-2 Small上訓練的大型SAE中提取的特徵，並用合成混合域語料庫進行探測，我們的聚類恢復了啟發式的主題家族（重標點模式、語言和字母集群，以及類似代碼的模板），這些是通過解碼器餘弦相似性聚類無法恢復的。<lb/>一個標記直方圖基線達到了更高的整體純度，因此圖視圖的貢獻是互補而非主導的：它揭示了僅依賴標記頻率和解碼器權重視圖無法捕捉的結構關係。<lb/>聚類分配在圖構建超參數和隨機種子之間是穩定的。
2605.06494.author: Ruben Fernandez-Boullon et.al.
2605.06494.authors: Ruben Fernandez-Boullon, Pablo Magariños-Docampo, Javier Perez-Robles
